task.title: Validate a directory of Markdown files
task.shortdesc: Run md-validate validate with a directory path to validate all Markdown files in that directory and its subdirectories at once. Use this before a release or periodic quality review.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Run markdown-validator against an entire directory to validate multiple Markdown files at once and save a report for each file.

prereq.p: markdown-validator is installed and the virtual environment is active. See .

context.p: When you pass a directory path instead of a file path, markdown-validator scans every .md file in that directory recursively. It produces one report per file and a summary in the terminal.
A single failing file sets the exit code to 1 for the entire run. This is the behavior CI pipelines rely on to gate the merge.

step.cmd: Run the validate command with a directory path.

info.p: Replace docs/ with the path to the directory you want to scan and rules/tutorial.json with your team's rule set.

step.stepresult: The tool prints a report for each file it validates, followed by a summary of how many files passed and failed.

step.cmd: Review the summary at the end of the output.

info.p: Scroll up to find the per-file reports for the two failed files. The file path is shown at the top of each report.

step.cmd: To save per-file reports, add the --output option.

info.p: The tool creates one report file per validated Markdown file in the reports/ directory. This is useful for sharing results with your team or tracking quality over time. For more about saving reports, see Save validation reports to a directory.

result.p: Every Markdown file in the directory has been validated. Files with Required failures are identified in the terminal output and, if you used --output, in the saved report files.
